 {"id":"bd-c83ac","title":"EPIC: Integrate post_compact_reminder (pcr) into ACFS","description":"Integrate post_compact_reminder — a Claude Code hook that reminds Claude to re-read AGENTS.md after context compaction.

BACKGROUND:
- Repo: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/post_compact_reminder
- Script: claude-post-compact-reminder (installed to ~/.local/bin/)
- Installer: install-post-compact-reminder.sh (2749 lines)
- Tech: Pure Bash 4.0+, requires jq, python3
- Version: 1.2.5
- License: MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic Rider

WHAT IT DOES:
- Hooks into Claude Code's SessionStart event with matcher: "compact"
- After context compaction, outputs a mandatory reminder to re-read AGENTS.md
- Four built-in templates: minimal, detailed, checklist, default
- All templates require confirmation before proceeding
- Atomic settings.json modifications with backups
- Self-updating capability
- Comprehensive health checks (--status, --doctor)

WHY IT MATTERS FOR FLYWHEEL:
- Context compaction is the #1 cause of Claude "going rogue" — forgetting project rules
- After compaction, Claude loses all memory of AGENTS.md conventions
- This hook forces Claude to re-read rules before continuing work
- Critical safety mechanism for long coding sessions
- Complements dcg (destructive command guard) as a behavioral guardrail

CURRENT STATE IN ACFS: Not integrated at all.

NOTE: This is a relatively simple integration since it's a Claude Code hook, not a standalone tool. But it's critically important for agent safety. It should be offered during acfs services-setup and installed via the installer. The hook script is lightweight (~60-90 lines at runtime).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:38:56.473006129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:23.609575Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:23.609575Z","close_reason":"done","closed_by_session":"SandyCitadel","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c83ac","depends_on_id":"bd-s9a3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:39:27.454207204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cadzg","title":"EPIC: Integrate process_triage (pt) into ACFS","description":"Integrate process_triage — a Bayesian-inspired intelligent process detection and cleanup tool for developer workstations.

BACKGROUND:
- Repo: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/process_triage
- Binaries: pt (bash wrapper), pt-core (Rust inference engine)
- Tech: Rust 2021 edition, 8-crate workspace (pt-core, pt-common, pt-config, pt-math, pt-redact, pt-bundle, pt-telemetry, pt-report)
- Install: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash
- Version: 2.0.4
- License: MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic Rider

WHAT IT DOES:
- Bayesian four-state classification: Useful, Useful-but-bad, Abandoned, Zombie
- Evidence-based scoring using process type, age, CPU/IO activity, TTY state, parent PID, memory
- Confidence levels with formal safety gates (min_posterior, max_kills, max_blast_radius, fdr_budget)
- Protected process lists (never kills systemd, postgres, ssh, etc.)
- Staged kill signals: SIGTERM → wait → SIGKILL
- Learning from user decisions, shadow mode for calibration
- Agent-friendly robot/agent subcommands with JSON output

WHY IT MATTERS FOR FLYWHEEL:
- Multi-agent coding sessions spawn many background processes that accumulate
- Prevents resource exhaustion (the tool was born when 23 stuck bun test workers + 31GB Hyprland brought a 64-core machine to its knees)
- Agent-safe: formal safety gates prevent killing critical processes
- Telemetry and blame attribution help understand which agents consume most resources

CURRENT STATE IN ACFS: Not integrated at all.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:38:53.356574581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:23.609575Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:23.609575Z","close_reason":"done","closed_by_session":"SandyCitadel","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cadzg","depends_on_id":"bd-s9a3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:39:05.007938500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cax6","title":"[Phase 6] DCG Website Commands Reference Integration","description":"## Phase 6: Website Commands Reference Integration

Add DCG commands to the commands reference page.

### Background

The commands reference page (apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx) lists all available commands organized by category. It's the go-to reference for users wanting to know what commands are available. Currently, DCG commands are not listed.

### Why This Matters

Users need a quick reference for DCG commands. The commands page is searchable and organized, making it easy to find what you need. Without DCG here, users have to dig through external documentation.

### Implementation Details

Add multiple DCG command entries to the COMMANDS array:
- dcg (main command)
- dcg test (check if command would be blocked)
- dcg packs (list protection packs)
- dcg allow-once (bypass with short code)
- dcg doctor (check installation)

### Files to Modify

- apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx: Add DCG commands to COMMANDS array

### Acceptance Criteria

- All DCG commands appear in commands reference
- Commands are in "stack" category
- Each has accurate description and example
- Links to tools/dcg page","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:48:54.130709518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:16:15.011786498Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:16:15.011786498Z","close_reason":"Commands reference already includes DCG entries (dcg, dcg test, packs, allow-once, doctor)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cax6","depends_on_id":"bd-7ifk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T03:52:40.111651957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-cfk","title":"Write security best practices documentation page","description":"## Task

Create a documentation page explaining our security recommendations and the Google SSO strategy.

## Location

`apps/web/app/docs/security/page.tsx` or `apps/web/content/security.mdx`

## Content Outline

### 1. Introduction
- Why security matters for vibe coding
- The "one account to rule them all" philosophy
- This guide's scope (account security, not VPS hardening)

### 2. The Google SSO Strategy

#### Why We Recommend It
- Single identity across services
- Fewer passwords = fewer weak points
- Google's security infrastructure protects everything
- Easy recovery through Google

#### How It Works
```
Your Google Account
    ├── Tailscale (VPS access)
    ├── Claude.ai (coding agent)
    ├── ChatGPT/Codex (coding agent)
    ├── Gemini (coding agent - native)
    ├── Vercel (deployment)
    └── Supabase (database)
```

#### The Trade-Off
- Single point of failure: if Google compromised, everything is
- Mitigated by: making Google account extremely secure

### 3. Securing Your Google Account

#### Essential Steps (Do These Now)
1. **Enable 2-Step Verification**
   - Go to: https://myaccount.google.com/signinoptions/two-step-verification
   - Use authenticator app, not SMS
   
2. **Review Recovery Options**
   - Recovery email: set a secondary email
   - Recovery phone: set if comfortable
   
3. **Check Connected Apps**
   - Review: https://myaccount.google.com/permissions
   - Remove any you don't recognize

#### Advanced Steps (Highly Recommended)
1. **Use a Strong, Unique Password**
   - Generated by password manager
   - Never used elsewhere
   
2. **Enable Advanced Protection Program**
   - For high-risk users (developers count!)
   - https://landing.google.com/advancedprotection/
   - Requires security keys

3. **Use Security Keys**
   - Hardware keys (YubiKey, Google Titan)
   - Strongest form of 2FA
   - Required for Advanced Protection

### 4. Services Without Google SSO

Some services don't support Google SSO:
- **GitHub**: Email + password (use strong, unique password)
- **Cloudflare**: Email + password

For these:
- Use your primary Google email address
- Generate strong password with password manager
- Enable their native 2FA

### 5. Password Manager Recommendation

If not already using one:
- **1Password**: Best overall
- **Bitwarden**: Best free option
- **Apple Keychain**: Good if Apple ecosystem

Use it for:
- GitHub password
- Cloudflare password
- Any service without Google SSO

### 6. Quick Security Checklist

```
[ ] Google 2-Step Verification enabled
[ ] Google recovery options set
[ ] Using authenticator app (not SMS)
[ ] GitHub 2FA enabled
[ ] Strong, unique passwords for non-SSO services
[ ] Using a password manager
```

### 7. What If Google Gets Compromised?

Steps to take immediately:
1. Change Google password from a trusted device
2. Revoke all connected app permissions
3. Check for unauthorized logins
4. Re-authenticate all SSO services
5. Enable Advanced Protection Program

## Design Notes

- Use callout boxes for important warnings
- Include screenshots for Google settings
- Make checklist interactive (can check off items)
- Mobile-friendly layout","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkCastle","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:01:16.353430Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:36:17.010786Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T22:36:17.010786Z","close_reason":"Enhanced security docs with password manager recommendations and account recovery steps","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cfk","depends_on_id":"bd-4ey","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:03:25.269753Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cgkx","title":"Preserve key/value structure when redacting session content","description":"sanitize_content() currently replaces the entire key/value substring (e.g. {"password": "secret"} -> {"[REDACTED]"}), which destroys context and can make snippets unreadable. Update the sed-based redaction for common key/value secrets to preserve the key, delimiter, and quotes while replacing only the secret value (e.g. {"password": "[REDACTED]"}, password="[REDACTED]").","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T05:26:24.684857Z","updated_at":"2025-12-29T05:32:39.981236Z","closed_at":"2025-12-29T05:32:39.981236Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cgsw","title":"Add DCG migration for existing ACFS users","description":"## Task: Add DCG migration for existing ACFS users

### What to Do

Handle the case where existing ACFS users upgrade and don't have DCG installed.

### Scenario

A user installed ACFS before DCG was added. When they run `acfs update`, the update script should:
1. Detect that DCG is not installed
2. Offer to install it
3. Register the hook after installation

### Location

File: /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/scripts/lib/update.sh

### Code to Add

In update_stack() function, add migration check:

```bash
# DCG Migration: Install if not present
if ! command -v dcg &>/dev/null; then
    echo ""
    log_info "DCG (Destructive Command Guard) is now part of the ACFS stack"
    log_info "It protects your codebase by blocking dangerous commands"
    echo ""

    if confirm_action "Install DCG now?" "yes"; then
        run_cmd "Installing DCG" update_run_verified_installer dcg --easy-mode
        if command -v dcg &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
            run_cmd "Registering DCG hook" dcg install --force 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    else
        log_detail "Skipping DCG installation"
        log_detail "To install later: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/dangerous_command_guard/main/install.sh | bash"
    fi
fi
```

### Why This Matters

- Existing users shouldn't miss out on safety features
- Automatic offering feels helpful, not intrusive
- User can decline if they don't want DCG
- Clear instructions for manual install later

### User Experience

1. User runs: `acfs update --stack`
2. Script detects DCG not installed
3. Shows message explaining DCG value
4. Prompts: "Install DCG now? [Y/n]"
5. If yes: Installs and registers hook
6. If no: Shows manual install command

### Testing

1. On system without DCG: Should prompt during update
2. On system with DCG: Should not prompt, just update
3. Decline installation: Should show manual command
4. Accept installation: Should install and register hook","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:34:06.212673296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:24.324883468Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:17:24.324883468Z","close_reason":"Added DCG migration prompt for missing installs in update_stack; uses verified installer with --easy-mode and main install URL, registers hook.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cgsw","depends_on_id":"bd-4olg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T04:34:12.594763758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-cgsw","author":"ubuntu","text":"FIX NEEDED: Change 'dangerous_command_guard' to 'destructive_command_guard' in curl URL","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:30:18Z"},{"id":33,"issue_id":"bd-cgsw","author":"ubuntu","text":"ALSO FIX: Branch should be 'master' not 'main' in the curl URL","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:44:13Z"}]}

 # ============================================================

-ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256="3f694a41d0056ba13ef5436eb5cc69226b5c99fe28c5d0df6eb3ff2e62705782"

 ACFS_MODULES_IN_ORDER=(
   "base.system"

@@ -788,6 +788,88 @@ _stack_run_verified_installer() {
     _stack_run_verified_installer_with_env "$tool" "" "$@"
 }
 
+_stack_fsfs_linux_target_triple() {
+    local arch=""
+
+    [[ "$(uname -s 2>/dev/null)" == "Linux" ]] || return 1
+    arch="$(uname -m 2>/dev/null || true)"
+
+    case "$arch" in
+        x86_64|amd64)
+            printf '%s\n' "x86_64-unknown-linux-musl"
+            ;;
+        aarch64|arm64)
+            printf '%s\n' "aarch64-unknown-linux-musl"
+            ;;
+        *)
+            return 1
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+_stack_resolve_fsfs_latest_version() {
+    if [[ -n "${ACFS_FSFS_VERSION:-}" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$ACFS_FSFS_VERSION"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local latest_url="https://api.github.com/repos/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch/releases/latest"
+    local tag=""
+    tag="$(curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 60 -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" "$latest_url" 2>/dev/null \
+        | sed -n 's/.*"tag_name"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' \
+        | head -n 1 || true)"
+
+    [[ -n "$tag" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$tag"
+}
+
+_stack_fetch_fsfs_artifact_checksum() {
+    local checksum_url="$1"
+    local checksum=""
+
+    checksum="$(curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 60 "$checksum_url" 2>/dev/null \
+        | awk 'NR == 1 { print $1 }' || true)"
+    [[ "$checksum" =~ ^[0-9A-Fa-f]{64}$ ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "${checksum,,}"
+}
+
+_stack_run_fsfs_installer() {
+    local -a fsfs_args=("$@")
+    local fsfs_target=""
+    local fsfs_version=""
+    local fsfs_version_bare=""
+    local fsfs_artifact_url=""
+    local fsfs_checksum=""
+
+    if [[ "$(uname -s 2>/dev/null)" == "Linux" ]]; then
+        if ! fsfs_target="$(_stack_fsfs_linux_target_triple 2>/dev/null)"; then
+            log_warn "FrankenSearch Linux binary artifact unavailable for this architecture; skipping source-build fallback"
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        if ! fsfs_version="$(_stack_resolve_fsfs_latest_version 2>/dev/null)"; then
+            log_warn "Unable to resolve FrankenSearch release; skipping source-build fallback"
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        fsfs_version_bare="${fsfs_version#v}"
+        fsfs_artifact_url="https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch/releases/download/${fsfs_version}/fsfs-lite-${fsfs_version_bare}-${fsfs_target}.tar.xz"
+        if ! fsfs_checksum="$(_stack_fetch_fsfs_artifact_checksum "${fsfs_artifact_url}.sha256" 2>/dev/null)"; then
+            log_warn "Unable to verify FrankenSearch lite artifact checksum; skipping source-build fallback"
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        fsfs_args+=(
+            --version "$fsfs_version"
+            --artifact-url "$fsfs_artifact_url"
+            --checksum "$fsfs_checksum"
+        )
+        log_detail "Using FrankenSearch Linux lite artifact: $fsfs_artifact_url"
+    fi
+
+    _stack_run_verified_installer fsfs "${fsfs_args[@]}"
+}
+
 _stack_run_installer() {
     if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
         log_warn "_stack_run_installer requires a tool name"

+update_fsfs_linux_target_triple() {
+    local arch=""
+
+    [[ "$(uname -s 2>/dev/null)" == "Linux" ]] || return 1
+    arch="$(uname -m 2>/dev/null || true)"
+
+    case "$arch" in
+        x86_64|amd64)
+            printf '%s\n' "x86_64-unknown-linux-musl"
+            ;;
+        aarch64|arm64)
+            printf '%s\n' "aarch64-unknown-linux-musl"
+            ;;
+        *)
+            return 1
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+update_resolve_fsfs_latest_version() {
+    if [[ -n "${ACFS_FSFS_VERSION:-}" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$ACFS_FSFS_VERSION"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local latest_url="https://api.github.com/repos/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch/releases/latest"
+    local tag=""
+    tag="$(curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 60 -H "Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json" "$latest_url" 2>/dev/null \
+        | sed -n 's/.*"tag_name"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"\([^"]*\)".*/\1/p' \
+        | head -n 1 || true)"
+
+    [[ -n "$tag" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$tag"
+}
+
+update_fetch_fsfs_artifact_checksum() {
+    local checksum_url="$1"
+    local checksum=""
+
+    checksum="$(curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 60 "$checksum_url" 2>/dev/null \
+        | awk 'NR == 1 { print $1 }' || true)"
+    [[ "$checksum" =~ ^[0-9A-Fa-f]{64}$ ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "${checksum,,}"
+}
+
+update_run_fsfs_installer() {
+    local -a fsfs_args=("$@")
+    local fsfs_target=""
+    local fsfs_version=""
+    local fsfs_version_bare=""
+    local fsfs_artifact_url=""
+    local fsfs_checksum=""
+
+    if [[ "$(uname -s 2>/dev/null)" == "Linux" ]]; then
+        if ! fsfs_target="$(update_fsfs_linux_target_triple 2>/dev/null)"; then
+            log_to_file "FrankenSearch Linux lite artifact unsupported for this architecture"
+            echo "FrankenSearch Linux binary artifact unavailable for this architecture; skipping source-build fallback" >&2
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        if ! fsfs_version="$(update_resolve_fsfs_latest_version 2>/dev/null)"; then
+            log_to_file "FrankenSearch release resolution failed; skipping source-build fallback"
+            echo "Unable to resolve FrankenSearch release; skipping source-build fallback" >&2
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        fsfs_version_bare="${fsfs_version#v}"
+        fsfs_artifact_url="https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch/releases/download/${fsfs_version}/fsfs-lite-${fsfs_version_bare}-${fsfs_target}.tar.xz"
+        if ! fsfs_checksum="$(update_fetch_fsfs_artifact_checksum "${fsfs_artifact_url}.sha256" 2>/dev/null)"; then
+            log_to_file "FrankenSearch lite artifact checksum unavailable; skipping source-build fallback"
+            echo "Unable to verify FrankenSearch lite artifact checksum; skipping source-build fallback" >&2
+            return 1
+        fi
+
+        fsfs_args+=(
+            --version "$fsfs_version"
+            --artifact-url "$fsfs_artifact_url"
+            --checksum "$fsfs_checksum"
+        )
+        log_to_file "FrankenSearch Linux lite artifact selected: $fsfs_artifact_url"
+    fi
+
+    update_run_verified_installer fsfs "${fsfs_args[@]}"
+}
